resources.string: Learn how to trigger an action at a given cycle.
Click every second on the button for 5 seconds. Try to stay consistent to get a higher score!

Welcome to the Timer reached condition tutorial! \n\nHere, you will try to trigger an action at a given cycle. You can try to beat the game first, or skip directly to the scenario configuration to start automating it.
The Trigger Events are in a separate list from the Screen Events, click on Trigger Event icon to open it.
Now, click on create to create a new Trigger Event.

As you can see, the Trigger Event structure is roughly the same as the Screen Event. We need first to declare when we want to execute our actions. \n\nClick on the Trigger Conditions field to open the condition list.
For now, the list is empty. Click on the create button to create a new Trigger Condition.
To beat the game, we need to click at a given time, so select the Timer Reached condition type.

The timer reached condition is considered fulfilled after a given amount of time. We want to click every second, so let\'s use 1000 ms. \n\nThe tutorial will continue once you input 1000 in the \"After\" box.

By default, Timer Reached Conditions are triggered only once, but here we want to click every second, not just one second after start. \n\nClick on the checkbox to restart the condition timer once it has been reached.

Our condition is now configured to be fulfilled every second. Keep in mind that your other scenario elements will still need to be processed, so a scenario with plenty of enabled Events can delay this condition. \n\nClick on save to close the dialog and return to the Trigger Condition list.
That\'s all the conditions we need. Close the dialog to return to the Trigger Event configuration.
Now the event knows when to trigger its actions, but we still need to define what to trigger. Click on the Action field to display the action list.

When the condition is fulfilled (so, every second), we need to click on the game button. Simply create a new Click action and set its position on the game button. \n\nTutorial will proceed once you return to the event list with your created event.
Our Trigger Event knows when to act and what to do, we can now save our scenario and try to beat the game. \n\nTutorial will proceed once you save the scenario.
Now restart the game, start your scenario and watch Klick\'r beat the game for you!
